# Amazon SNS Connector - Full Documentation

# Amazon SNS 4.8 Additional Configuration Information

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## IAM User Permissions

To use Amazon SNS Connector for an AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) user, that user must:

- Be a trusted entity for a specific role with rights to perform the Amazon Security Token Service
  `AssumeRole` action.
- Have at least an `AmazonSNSReadOnlyAccess` policy to perform all SNS Connector read operations.
- Have an `AmazonSNSFullAccess` policy to perform all SNS Connector operations.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Amazon SNS 4.8 Connector Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Common use cases for the connector include:

- Sending SNS messages to Amazon SQS queues
- Sending Amazon SNS messages to HTTP/HTTPS endpoints

## Send Amazon SNS Messages to Amazon SQS Queues

Amazon SNS works closely with Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS). By using Amazon SNS and
Amazon SQS together, messages can be delivered to applications that:

- Require immediate notification of an event.
- Are persisted in an Amazon SQS queue for other applications to process later.

When you subscribe an Amazon SQS queue to an Amazon SNS topic, you can publish a message to the
topic. Amazon SNS then sends an Amazon SQS message to the subscribed queue.

Use AWS Management Console to simplify the process of subscribing an Amazon SQS queue to an Amazon
SNS topic.

## Send Amazon SNS Messages to HTTP/HTTPS Endpoints

You can use Amazon SNS to send notification messages to one or more HTTP or HTTPS endpoints. When
you subscribe an endpoint to am Amazon SNS topic, you can publish a notification to the topic.
Amazon SNS then sends an HTTP POST request that delivers the contents of the notification to the
subscribed endpoint.

## Example: Use the Connector to Send SNS Messages to Amazon SQS Queues

The following diagram shows an example flow for this use case:

![Send messages to SQS](_images/amazon-sns-use-case-flow.png)

Before you try the example, access Anypoint Studio, and verify that the Mule Palette displays
entries for **Amazon SNS** and **Amazon SQS**. If not, follow the instructions in
[Install the Connector in Studio Using Exchange](amazon-sns-connector-studio#install-connector) to
install both connectors.

To configure a Mule app that uses a connector to send Amazon SNS messages to Amazon SQS queues:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Anypoint Studio.
2.  In the Mule Palette, search for **HTTP**, and select the **Listener** operation:

    ![Select listener](_images/amazon-sns-select-listener.png)

3.  Drag the **Listener** operation to the canvas.
4.  In the **Listener** configuration, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to
    add a global element.
5.  Accept the defaults.
6.  In the **Listener** configuration **General** tab, set the **Display Name** field to `HTTP` and
    the **Path** field to\`/''.

    ![HTTP properties](_images/amazon-sns-http-properties.png)

7.  In the Mule Palette, search for **SNS** and select the **Publish** operation:

    ![Publish selection](_images/amazon-sns-publish-selection.png)

8.  Drag the **Publish** operation to the right of the **HTTP Listener** component.
9.  In the **Publish** configuration, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to
    add a global element.
10. Configure the global element as follows:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><p>Name used to reference the configuration</p></td><td><p><code>Amazon_SNS_Configuration</code> or another name</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Key</strong></p></td><td><p>Alphanumeric text string that uniquely identifies the user who owns the account.</p></td><td><p>AWS Access Key</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Key</strong></p></td><td><p>Key that plays the role of a password</p></td><td><p>AWS secret key</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Region Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><p>Queue region.</p></td><td><p>The queue region</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows a sample global element for the Publish operation:

    ![SNS global configuration](_images/amazon-sns-global-config.png)

11. Select the **Configuration XML** tab to view the corresponding XML:

    ```xml
    <sns:config name="Amazon_SNS_Configuration"
      doc:name="Amazon SNS configuration" >
      <sns:basic-connection
        accessKey="EXAMPLEACCESSKEY"
        secretKey="EXAMPLESECRETKEY" />
    </sns:config>
    ```

12. Select the **Global Elements** tab.
13. Select the **Amazon SNS configuration (Configuration)** and click **Edit**.
14. Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the SNS instance.
    - If the connection is successful, click **OK** to save the configuration.
    - Otherwise, review or correct any incorrect parameters, and test again.

15. Go back to the flow and select **Publish**.
16. Configure parameters in General Tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Display Name</strong></p></td><td><p><code>Publish</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the publish operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3"><p><strong>Basic Settings section</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connector configuration</strong></p></td><td><p><code>Amazon_SNS_configuration</code></p></td><td><p>The reference name to the global element you created.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3"><p><strong>Target Platform section</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic arn</strong></p></td><td><p><code>arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:TopicName</code></p></td><td><p>Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the topic you want to publish to.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target arn</strong></p></td><td></td><td><p>Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the platform endpoint you want to publish to.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Phone number</strong></p></td><td></td><td><p>The phone number of the device you want to publish to.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3"><p><strong>Message Parameters</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message</strong></p></td><td><p><code>`Hello World!`</code></p></td><td><p>Message Body</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows a sample configuration for the Publish Message operation (General
    Tab):

    ![SNS publish message props](_images/amazon-sns-publish-message-general.png)

17. Configure parameters in Advanced Tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="3"><p><strong>Message Parameters</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subject</strong></p></td><td><p><code>Testing publish to queue</code></p></td><td><p>Subject line when the message is delivered to email endpoints.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message structure</strong></p></td><td><p><code>JSON</code></p></td><td><p>Message structure that will be different for each protocol.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message attributes</strong></p></td><td></td><td><p>Amazon SNS message attributes, as described in the <a href="https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/welcome.html">Amazon Simple Notification Service Developer Guide</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3"><p><strong>FIFO Parameters</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Group ID</strong></p></td><td></td><td><p>Token that specifies that a message belongs to a specific message group. Required when publishing to a FIFO topic.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Deduplication ID</strong></p></td><td></td><td><p>Token used for the deduplication of messages when publishing to a FIFO topic. Allows you to overwrite the value generated by Amazon SNS. Required when content-based deduplication is disabled.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows a sample configuration for the Publish Message operation (Advanced
    Tab):

    ![SNS publish message props](_images/amazon-sns-publish-message-advanced.png)

18. Select the **Configuration XML** tab to view the corresponding XML:

    ```xml
    <sns:publish doc:name="Publish" doc:id="276aca8a-334a-4a04-a2ec-8ca4b21743c5"
        config-ref="Amazon_SNS_SNS_configuration"
        topicArn="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:TopicName"
        subject="Testing publish to queue"
        messageStructure="JSON">
        <sns:message ><![CDATA[#['{"default": "Hello world!"}']]]></sns:message>
    </sns:publish>
    ```

    The `default` JSON key has a special meaning for SNS. The keyâs value is sent as a message to
    every subscriber, regardless of the protocol used. You must specify this key for the Publish
    Message operation. You can optionally specify other top-level keys that define the message to
    send to a specific transport protocol, such as HTTP or SQS. For more information, see
    [Publish](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_Publish.html#API_Publish_RequestParameters)
    in the Amazon Simple Notification Service documentation.

### Display the Data Processed by the Publish Operation

Using the same example, add a Logger component to display the data processed by the Publish
operation on the Mule console.

1.  In the Mule Palette, search for the **Logger** component and drag it to the right of the Amazon
    SNS Connector (**Publish**).
2.  Configure the **Logger** component as follows:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Display Name</strong></p></td><td><p><code>Logger</code> or another name</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message</strong></p></td><td><p><code>Message: #[payload]</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Level</strong></p></td><td><p><code>INFO</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    ![SNS logger](_images/amazon-sns-logger.png)

### Receive SNS Messages

Using the same example, add a second flow that enables Amazon SQS to receive messages published by
Amazon SNS:

1.  In the Mule Palette, search for **Flow** and drag the **Flow** component to the canvas.
2.  In the Mule Palette, search for **SQS**, select the **Receive messages** operation, and drag it
    to the left of the **Flow** component.
3.  Select the new component.
4.  Click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to add a new Amazon SQS Connector
    global element.
5.  Configure the global element as follows:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>A name by which to refer to the configuration</p></td><td><p><code>Amazon_SQS_Configuration</code> or another name</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Key</p></td><td><p>Alphanumeric text string that uniquely identifies the user who owns the account</p></td><td><p>AWS access key</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Secret Key</p></td><td><p>Key that plays the role of a password.</p></td><td><p>AWS secret key</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Region Endpoint</p></td><td><p>Queue region.</p></td><td><p>The queue region</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Test Queue ARN</p></td><td><p>Queue ARN which will be used in the next step to test the connection</p></td><td><p>Test queue ARN in the following format - <code>arn:aws:sqs:region:account-id:queue-name</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows a sample global element for the Receive messages operation:

    ![SNS SQS config](_images/amazon-sns-sqs-global-config.png)

6.  Select the **Configuration XML** tab to view the corresponding XML:

    ```xml
    <sqs:config name="Amazon_SQS_Configuration"
      doc:name="Amazon SQS Configuration" >
    	<sqs:basic-connection
      region="us_east_1"
      accessKey="EXAMPLEACCESSKEY"
      secretKey="EXAMPLESECRETKEY"
      testQueueArn="arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:QueueName"/>
    </sqs:config>
    ```

7.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the SQS instance:
    - If the connection is successful, click **OK** to save it.
    - If the connection is not successful, review or correct any incorrect parameters, and then test
      again.

8.  In the properties editor of Amazon SQS Connector, configure the remaining parameters:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="2"><p>Basic Settings section</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p><code>Amazon SQS (Streaming)</code> or another name</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration*</p></td><td><p><code>Amazon_SQS_Configuration</code> (the reference name to the global element you created)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Queue url</p></td><td><p>Queue URL in the following format - <code><a href="https://sqs.region.amazonaws.com/account-id/queue-name">https://sqs.region.amazonaws.com/account-id/queue-name</a></code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Other fields in the <strong>General</strong> group</p></td><td><p>Default values</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    Verify that the SQS queue in the configuration is **subscribed** to the SNS topic.

    For example:

    ![SNS SQS config](_images/amazon-sns-sqs-config.png)

9.  Select the **Configuration XML** tab to view the corresponding XML:

    ```xml
    <sqs:receivemessages
      doc:name="Amazon SQS (Streaming)"
      queueUrl="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/queue-url"
      config-ref="Amazon_SQS_Configuration"/>
    ```

10. Add a **Logger** scope after Amazon SQS Connector to print the data passed by the **Receive**
    operation in the Mule console. Configure the **Logger** as follows:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Display Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>Display Message</code> or another name</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>Received Message: #[payload]</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Level</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>INFO</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

11. Save the project.

To run the project as a Mule app:

1.  Right-click the project in Package Explorer and select **Run As > Mule Application**.
2.  Open a web browser, enter the URL `http://localhost:8081/`, and check the response.

    The logger shows the published message ID on the browser and the received message on the Mule
    console.

### Demo Mule Application XML Code

Paste this code into your XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example use case into your
Mule application. If needed, change the values to reflect your environment.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:sqs="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sqs"
	xmlns:sns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sns"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sns http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sns/current/mule-sns.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sqs http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sqs/current/mule-sqs.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<sns:config name="Amazon_SNS_configuration" doc:name="Amazon SNS configuration">
		<sns:basic-connection accessKey="EXAMPLEACCESSKEY" secretKey="EXAMPLESECRETKEY" />
	</sns:config>
	<sqs:config name="Amazon_SQS_Configuration" doc:name="Amazon SQS Configuration">
		<sqs:basic-connection accessKey="EXAMPLEACCESSKEY" secretKey="EXAMPLESECRETKEY"
		testQueueArn="arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:QueueName"/>
	</sqs:config>
	<flow name="sns-connector-exampleFlow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/"/>
		<sns:publish doc:name="Publish" config-ref="Amazon_SNS_configuration"
		topicArn="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:TopicName"
		subject="Testing publish to queue"
		messageStructure="JSON">
			<sns:message><![CDATA[#['{
	"default": "Hello World!"
}']]]></sns:message>
		</sns:publish>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" message="Message: #[payload]"/>
	</flow>
	<flow name="sns-connector-exampleFlow1">
		<sqs:receivemessages doc:name="Amazon SQS (Streaming)" config-ref="Amazon_SQS_Configuration"
		queueUrl="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/queue-url"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" message="Received Message: #[payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

# Amazon SNS 4.8 Connector Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Release Notes:
[Amazon SNS Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/amazon-sns-connector-release-notes-mule-4)

## Configurations

---

### Default Configuration

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#config_basic">Basic</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#config_role">Role</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Globally Defined Default Topic ARN.</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the instance expires at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Basic

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Session Token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The session token provided by AWS Security Token Service (STS).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Test Topic</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The topic used to test the connection.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of time to wait (in milliseconds) when initially establishing a connection before the connector gives up and times out. A value of 0 means infinity and is not recommended.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>50000</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the maximum number of allowed open HTTP connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of time to wait (in milliseconds) for data to be transferred over an established, open connection before the connection times out. A value of 0 means infinity and is not recommended.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>50000</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The access key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The secret key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Try Default AWSCredentials Provider Chain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set this field to <code>true</code> to obtain credentials from the AWS environment. See <a href="https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-java/latest/developer-guide/credentials.html">Supplying and Receiving AWS Credentials</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Region Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set the topic region endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>US_EAST_1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom Service Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set a custom service endpoint. Useful when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a VPC endpoint. For local connections, set this field to <code>http://localhost:8000/</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the proxy host, including the protocol. Must be in the format <code>http://&lt;host&gt;</code> or <code>https://&lt;host&gt;</code> (for example, <code>https://localhost</code>).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the proxy port. This parameter is required if a proxy host is provided.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy username.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy password.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Domain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy domain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Workstation</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy workstation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Role

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Role ARN</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Role ARN uniquely identifies role to assume to gain cross account access.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom STS Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set a custom STS endpoint. Useful when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a VPC endpoint. The custom STS endpoint might not work if <code>DefaultAWSCredentialsProviderChain</code> is used. Instead, use AWS environment variables such as <code>AWS_STS_REGIONAL_ENDPOINTS</code>, <code>AWS_REGION</code>, and <code>AWS_ENDPOINT_URL</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>External ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A unique identifier that might be required when you assume a role in another account. If the administrator of the account to which the role belongs provides an external ID, then provide that value in this field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Test Topic ARN</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The topic used to test the connection.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of time to wait (in milliseconds) when initially establishing a connection before the connector gives up and times out. A value of 0 means infinity and is not recommended.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>50000</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the maximum number of allowed open HTTP connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of time to wait (in milliseconds) for data to be transferred over an established, open connection before the connection times out. A value of 0 means infinity and is not recommended.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>50000</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The access key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The secret key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Try Default AWSCredentials Provider Chain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set this field to <code>true</code> to obtain credentials from the AWS environment. See: <a href="https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-java/latest/developer-guide/credentials.html">Supplying and Receiving AWS Credentials</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Region Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set the topic region endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>US_EAST_1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom Service Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set a custom service endpoint. Useful when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a VPC endpoint. For local connections, set this field to <code>http://localhost:8000/</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the proxy host, including the protocol. Must be in the format <code>http://&lt;host&gt;</code> or <code>https://&lt;host&gt;</code> (for example, <code>https://localhost</code>).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the proxy port. This parameter is required if a proxy host is provided.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy username.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy password.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Domain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy domain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Workstation</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The optional proxy workstation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

- [Add Permission](#addPermission)
- [Confirm Subscription](#confirmSubscription)
- [Create Platform Application](#createPlatformApplication)
- [Create Platform Endpoint](#createPlatformEndpoint)
- [Create Topic](#createTopic)
- [Delete Endpoint](#deleteEndpoint)
- [Delete Platform Application](#deletePlatformApplication)
- [Delete Topic](#deleteTopic)
- [Get Endpoint Attributes](#getEndpointAttributes)
- [Get Platform Application Attributes](#getPlatformApplicationAttributes)
- [Get Subscription Attributes](#getSubscriptionAttributes)
- [Get Topic Attributes](#getTopicAttributes)
- [List Endpoints By Platform Application](#listEndpointsByPlatformApplication)
- [List Platform Applications](#listPlatformApplications)
- [List Subscriptions](#listSubscriptions)
- [List Subscriptions By Topic](#listSubscriptionsByTopic)
- [List Topics](#listTopics)
- [Publish](#publish)
- [Remove Permission](#removePermission)
- [Set Endpoint Attributes](#setEndpointAttributes)
- [Set Platform Application Attributes](#setPlatformApplicationAttributes)
- [Set Subscription Attributes](#setSubscriptionAttributes)
- [Set Topic Attributes](#setTopicAttributes)
- [Subscribe](#subscribe)
- [Unsubscribe](#unsubscribe)

### Add Permission

`<sns:add-permission>`

Adds a statement to a topicâs access control policy, granting access for the specified AWS accounts
to the specified actions. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_AddPermission.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ARN of the topic whose access control policy you wish to modify.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Label</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A unique identifier for the new policy statement.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Account Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The AWS account IDs of the users (principals) who will be given access to the specified actions. The users must have AWS accounts, but do not need to be signed up for this service.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Action Names</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The action you want to allow for the specified principals.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Confirm Subscription

`<sns:confirm-subscription>`

The confirmSubscription action verifies an endpoint ownerâs intent to receive messages by validating
the token sent to the endpoint by an earlier Subscribe action. If the token is valid, the action
creates a new subscription and returns its Amazon Resource Name (ARN). This call requires an AWS
signature only when the AuthenticateOnUnsubscribe flag is set to `true`. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_ConfirmSubscription.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ARN of the topic for which you wish to confirm a subscription.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Short-lived token sent to an endpoint during the <code>Subscribe</code> action.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authenticate On Unsubscribe</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Disallows unauthenticated unsubscribes of the subscription. If the value of this parameter is <code>true</code> and the request has an AWS signature, then only the topic owner and the subscription owner can unsubscribe the endpoint. The unsubscribe action requires AWS authentication.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Create Platform Application

`<sns:create-platform-application>`

Creates a platform application object for one of the supported push notification services, such as
APNs (Apple Push Notification service) and GCM (Google Cloud Messaging), to which devices and mobile
apps may register. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_CreatePlatformApplication.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Application names must be made up of only uppercase and lowercase ASCII letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens, and periods, and must be between 1 and 256 characters long.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The following platforms are supported: ADM (Amazon Device Messaging), APNs (Apple Push Notification service), APNS_SANDBOX, and GCM (Google Cloud Messaging).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The list of attributes.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Create Platform Endpoint

`<sns:create-platform-endpoint>`

Creates an endpoint for a device and mobile app on one of the supported push notification services.
CreatePlatformEndpoint requires the PlatformApplicationArn that is returned from
CreatePlatformApplication. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_CreatePlatformEndpoint.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform Application Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>PlatformApplicationArn returned from CreatePlatformApplication is used to create a an endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique identifier created by the notification service for an app on a device. The specific name for Token will vary, depending on which notification service is being used. For example, when using APNs (Apple Push Notification service) as the notification service, you need the device token. Alternatively, when using GCM (Google Cloud Messaging) or ADM (Amazon Device Messaging), the device token equivalent is called the registration ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom User Data</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Arbitrary user data to associate with the endpoint. Amazon SNS does not use this data. The data must be in UTF-8 format and less than 2KB.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The list of attributes.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Create Topic

`<sns:create-topic>`

The CreateTopic action creates a topic to which notifications can be published. Users can create at
most 100 topics. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_CreateTopic.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the topic. Maximum 256 characters. Can include alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores. FIFO topic names must end with <code>.fifo</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Based Deduplication</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables content-based deduplication for FIFO topics. If you create a FIFO topic and set this parameter to <code>false</code>, you must specify a value for the <strong>Message Deduplication Id</strong> parameter for the <strong>Publish</strong> operation. When you set this parameter to <code>true</code>, Amazon SNS uses a SHA-256 hash to generate the message deduplication ID using the body of the message (but not the attributes of the message).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Delete Endpoint

`<sns:delete-endpoint>`

Deletes the endpoint for a device and mobile app from Amazon SNS. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_DeleteEndpoint.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Endpoint Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>EndpointArn of endpoint to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Delete Platform Application

`<sns:delete-platform-application>`

Deletes a platform application object for one of the supported push notification services, such as
APNs (Apple Push Notification service) and GCM (Google Cloud Messaging). See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_DeletePlatformApplication.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform Application Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>PlatformApplicationArn of platform application object to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Delete Topic

`<sns:delete-topic>`

The DeleteTopic action deletes a topic and all its subscriptions. Deleting a topic might prevent
some messages previously sent to the topic from being delivered to subscribers. This action is
idempotent, so deleting a topic that does not exist does not result in an error. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_DeleteTopic.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ARN of the topic you want to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Get Endpoint Attributes

`<sns:get-endpoint-attributes>`

Retrieves the endpoint attributes for a device on one of the supported push notification services,
such as GCM (Google Cloud Messaging) and APNs (Apple Push Notification service). See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_GetEndpointAttributes.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Endpoint Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>EndpointArn for GetEndpointAttributes input.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC

### Get Platform Application Attributes

`<sns:get-platform-application-attributes>`

Retrieves the attributes of the platform application object for the supported push notification
services, such as APNs (Apple Push Notification service)and GCM (Google Cloud Messaging). See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_GetPlatformApplicationAttributes.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform Application Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>PlatformApplicationArn for GetPlatformApplicationAttributesInput.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Get Subscription Attributes

`<sns:get-subscription-attributes>`

The GetSubscriptionAttributes action returns all of the properties of a subscription. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_GetSubscriptionAttributes.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subscription Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ARN of the subscription whose properties you want to get</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER

- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
