### Get Topic Attributes

`<sns:get-topic-attributes>`

The GetTopicAttributes action returns all of the properties of a topic. Topic properties returned
might differ based on the authorization of the user. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_GetTopicAttributes.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ARN of the topic whose properties you want to get.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### List Endpoints By Platform Application

`<sns:list-endpoints-by-platform-application>`

Lists the endpoints and endpoint attributes for devices in a supported push notification service,
such as GCM (Google Cloud Messaging) and APNs (Apple Push Notification service). See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_ListEndpointsByPlatformApplication.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ARN of the platform application.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of [<a href="#Endpoint">Endpoint</a>] payload and [<a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a>] attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR

### List Platform Applications

`<sns:list-platform-applications>`

Lists the platform application objects for the supported push notification services, such as APNs
(Apple Push Notification service)and GCM (Google Cloud Messaging). See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_ListPlatformApplications.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of [<a href="#PlatformApplication">Platform Application</a>] payload and [<a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a>] attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR

### List Subscriptions

`<sns:list-subscriptions>`

The ListSubscriptions action returns a list of the requesterâs subscriptions. Each call returns a
limited list of subscriptions, up to 100. If there are more subscriptions, a NextToken is also
returned. Use the NextToken parameter in a new ListSubscriptions call to get further results. See
the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_ListSubscriptions.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of [<a href="#Subscription">Subscription</a>] payload and [<a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a>] attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR

### List Subscriptions By Topic

`<sns:list-subscriptions-by-topic>`

The SubscriptionsByTopic action returns a list of the subscriptions to a specific topic. Each call
returns a limited list of subscriptions, up to 100. If there are more subscriptions, a NextToken is
also returned. Use the NextToken parameter in a new SubscriptionsByTopic call to get further
results. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_ListSubscriptionsByTopic.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ARN of the topic.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of [<a href="#Subscription">Subscription</a>] payload and [<a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a>] attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR

### List Topics

`<sns:list-topics>`

The ListTopics action returns a list of the requesterâs topics. Each call returns a limited list of
topics, up to 100. If there are more topics, a NextToken is also returned. Use the NextToken
parameter in a new ListTopics call to get further results. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_ListTopics.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of [String] payload and [<a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a>] attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR

### Publish

`<sns:publish>`

The Publish action sends a message to all of a topicâs subscribed endpoints. A returned messageId
indicates that the message was saved and Amazon SNS will attempt to deliver it to the topicâs
subscribers shortly. The format of the outgoing message to each subscribed endpoint depends on the
notification protocol selected. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_Publish.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>FIFO Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#FifoParameters">FIFO Parameters</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Parameters related to the FIFO topic.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The message you want to send to the topic.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subject</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional parameter to use as the "Subject" line when the message is delivered to email endpoints. This field will also be included, if present, in the standard JSON messages delivered to other endpoints.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Structure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set MessageStructure to <code>json</code> if you want to send a different message for each protocol.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Amazon SNS message attributes, as described in the <a href="https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/welcome.html">Amazon Simple Notification Service Developer Guide</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the topic you want to publish to. This field and the Target Arn field are mutually exclusive.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the platform endpoint you want to publish to. This field and the Topic Arn field are mutually exclusive.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Remove Permission

`<sns:remove-permission>`

The RemovePermission action removes a statement from a topicâs access control policy. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_RemovePermission.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ARN of the topic whose access control policy you wish to modify.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Label</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unique label of the statement you want to remove.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Set Endpoint Attributes

`<sns:set-endpoint-attributes>`

Sets the attributes for an endpoint for a device on one of the supported push notification services,
such as GCM (Google Cloud Messaging) and APNs (Apple Push Notification service). See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_SetEndpointAttributes.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Endpoint">Endpoint</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Container for the parameters to the SetEndpointAttributes operation.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Set Platform Application Attributes

`<sns:set-platform-application-attributes>`

Sets the attributes of the platform application object for the supported push notification services,
such as APNs (Apple Push Notification service) and GCM (Google Cloud Messaging). See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_SetPlatformApplicationAttributes.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform Application Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PlatformApplication">Platform Application</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Container for the parameters to the SetPlatformApplicationAttributes operation.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Set Subscription Attributes

`<sns:set-subscription-attributes>`

The SubscriptionAttributes action allows a subscription owner to set an attribute of the topic to a
new value. See the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_SetSubscriptionAttributes.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subscription Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ARN of the subscription.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The value of the attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Set Topic Attributes

`<sns:set-topic-attributes>`

The TopicAttributes action allows a topic owner to set an attribute of the topic to a new value. See
the
[Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_SetTopicAttributes.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ARN of the topic.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The value of the attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Subscribe

`<sns:subscribe>`

The Subscribe action prepares to subscribe an endpoint by sending the endpoint a confirmation
message. To actually create a subscription, the endpoint owner must call the confirmSubscription
action with the token from the confirmation message. Confirmation tokens are valid for three days.
See the [Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_Subscribe.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ARN of the topic you want to subscribe to.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Protocol</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li><li><p>EMAIL</p></li><li><p>EMAIL_JSON</p></li><li><p>SMS</p></li><li><p>SQS</p></li><li><p>APPLICATION</p></li><li><p>LAMBDA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The protocol you want to use. If it is a FIFO topic, then only Amazon SQS FIFO queues can be subscribers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The endpoint that you want to receive notifications. Endpoints vary by protocol:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP - The endpoint is an URL beginning with <code>http://</code>.</p></li><li><p>HTTPS - The endpoint is a URL beginning with <code>https://</code>.</p></li><li><p>EMAIL - The endpoint is an email address.</p></li><li><p>EMAIL_JSON - The endpoint is an email address.</p></li><li><p>SMS - The endpoint is a phone number of an SMS-enabled device.</p></li><li><p>SQS - The endpoint is the ARN of an Amazon SQS queue.</p></li><li><p>APPLICATION - The endpoint is the endpoint of a mobile app and device.</p></li><li><p>LAMBDA - The endpoint is the ARN of an AWS Lambda function.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

### Unsubscribe

`<sns:unsubscribe>`

The Unsubscribe action deletes a subscription. If the subscription requires authentication for
deletion, only the owner of the subscription or the topic owner can unsubscribe, and an AWS
signature is required. If the Unsubscribe call does not require authentication and the requester is
not the subscription owner, a final cancellation message is delivered to the endpoint, so that the
endpoint owner can easily resubscribe to the topic if the Unsubscribe request was unintended. See
the [Amazon SNS API Reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/api/API_Unsubscribe.html).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subscription Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ARN of the subscription to be deleted.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE
- SNS:INVALID_PARAMETER
- SNS:INTERNAL_ERROR
- SNS:CONNECTIVITY
- SNS:THROTTLED
- SNS:TOPIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:AUTHORIZATION_ERROR
- SNS:UNDEFINED_TOPIC
- SNS:NOT_FOUND
- SNS:SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SNS:ENDPOINT_DISABLED
- SNS:UNKNOWN
- SNS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SNS:PLATFORM_APPLICATION_DISABLED

## Types

### TLS

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>For servers, a trust store contains certificates of the trusted clients. For clients, a trust store contains certificates of the trusted servers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>For servers, a key store contains the private and public key of the server. For clients, a key store contains the private and public key of the client.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#standard-revocation-check">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#custom-ocsp-responder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#crl-file">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Validates that a certificate was revoked.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location of the trust store. The path is resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, no certificate validations will be performed, rendering connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location of the key store. The path is resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the key store contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key that should be used. If not defined, the first key in the file is used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server can not be reached or is busy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the trust store), if present.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How many reconnection attempts to make..</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>blocking</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>blocking</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></td><td><p>unsubscribe</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Request ID Attribute

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Request Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Endpoint

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Endpoint Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of instances that is initially be allowed to be kept in memory to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised when the buffer gets full. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised. A value lower than or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of instances to keep in memory. If more than that is required, content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Platform Application

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform Application Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Subscription

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Owner</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Protocol</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subscription Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic Arn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### FIFO Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Message Group Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Token that specifies where a message belongs.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Deduplication Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Token for deduplicating messages. This ID is mandatory when deduplication is not set in the topic level.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Amazon SNS 4.8 Studio Configuration

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To install and configure Anypoint Connector for Amazon SNS (Amazon SNS Connector) in Anypoint
Studio:

1.  Install the connector.
2.  Configure an input source for the connector.
3.  Configure the connector.

## Install the Connector in Studio Using Exchange

1.  In Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  Click the Exchange **(X)** icon in the Studio task bar.
3.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
4.  In Exchange, search for **Amazon SNS Connector**.
5.  Select **Amazon SNS Connector** and click **Add to project**.
6.  Follow the prompts to install the connector.

## Install the Connector in Studio

1.  In Studio, create a Mule Project.
2.  In the Mule Palette, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
3.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type the name of the connector in the search field.
4.  Click the connector name in **Available modules**.
5.  Click **Add**.
6.  Click **Finish**.

## Configure an Input Source

Configure an input source for the connector, such as an **HTTP Listener** or **Scheduler**.

## Configure the Connector

To use Amazon SNS Connector in your Mule app, configure a global Amazon SNS element that can be used
by all Amazon SNS Connectors in the application.

1.  Drag a connector operation into the Studio canvas.
2.  Click the **Global Elements** tab at the base of the canvas, and then click **Create**.
3.  In the **Choose Global Type** window, expand **Connector Configuration**, and click **Amazon SNS
    configuration**:

    ![Global Element Configuration Wizard](_images/amazon-sns-config-global-wizard.png)

4.  Click **OK**.
5.  Specify the global element properties:

    ![Global Element Properties](_images/amazon-sns-global-config.png)

6.  Configure the parameters as follows:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Name</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name by which the configuration is referenced</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Session Token</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Session token provided by AWS Security Token Service (STS)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Test Topic</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Topic used to test the connection</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Access Key</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alphanumeric text string that uniquely identifies the user who owns the account</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Secret Key</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key that plays the role of a password</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Try Default AWS Credentials Provider Chain</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Checkbox that controls whether temporary credentials should be used</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Region Endpoint</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Topic region endpoint</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Globally Defined Default Topic Arn</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Topic ARN to test the connectivity</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

7.  Retain the default entries for the **Proxy**, **Security**, and **Advanced** tabs.
8.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that the parameters of your global configuration are
    accurate, and that Mule is able to successfully connect to your instance of Amazon SNS.
9.  Click **OK**.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Upgrading and Migrating Amazon SNS Connector to Version 4.8

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Supported Upgrade Paths

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>From Version</th><th>To Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>4.7.x</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>4.8.x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Changes

### 4.8

- Amazon SNS Connector now supports passing an external ID in Role-based authentication.
- The AWS SDK version is upgraded to 2.35.3.

### 4.7

- Added support for FIFO topics, which provide strict message ordering and message deduplication
  with Amazon SQS FIFO queues.

### 4.6

- Added a new notification option. Use the **Phone number** parameter to send a text message to a
  specified phone number. This option is offered in addition to **Target Arn** and **Topic Arn**,
  which are the existing notification options.

### 4.5

- Added the ability to configure TLS and custom SNS and STS endpoints.
- Upgraded `aws-java-sdk` from 1.11.611 to 1.11.969.
- Upgraded `aws-connector-commons` from 2.0.0 to 2.3.0.

## Requirements

Ensure your system meets the following requirements before you perform the upgrade:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Application/Service</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Mule</p></td><td><p>4.1.1 and later</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Amazon Java SDK for RDS</p></td><td><p>v1.11.969</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Java</p></td><td><p>1.8.0_x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Anypoint Studio</p></td><td><p>7.1 and later</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

See the
[Amazon SNS Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/amazon-sns-connector-release-notes-mule-4)
for updated compatibility information for patch updates.

## Upgrade Prerequisites

Before you perform the upgrade, create a backup of your files, data, and configuration in case you
need to restore to the previous version.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Amazon SNS 4.8 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you manually code a Mule application in XML (either from the Studio XML editor or a text editor),
you must perform the following actions to access the connector:

1.  Add the namespace for the connector to your XML flow.
2.  Add dependency information to your Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.

In the Studio visual editor, when you drag a connector from the palette to the canvas, Studio
automatically populates the XML code with the connector namespace and schema location.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `<mule>` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sns
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sns/current/mule-sns.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the `<mule>` XML block:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0"?>

<mule xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
   xmlns:sns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sns"
   xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
   xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
   xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
   xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
   http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sns
   http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sns/current/mule-sns.xsd
   http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
   http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd">

<!-- Put your flows and configuration elements here -->

</mule>
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app. When you
code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access to this
connector.

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-amazon-sns-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information, access the connector in
[Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/) and click **Dependency Snippets**.

## Next Step

After completing your namespace and `pom.xml` file, you can try out
[Examples](amazon-sns-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Amazon SNS Connector 4.8 - Get Started

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Amazon SNS (Amazon SNS Connector) provides connectivity to the Amazon Simple
Notification Service (Amazon SNS) API, enabling you to build distributed web-enabled applications.
Mule apps can use Amazon SNS Connector to easily push real-time notification messages to interested
subscribers over multiple delivery protocols.

Release Notes:
[Amazon SNS Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/amazon-sns-connector-release-notes-mule-4)
Exchange:
[Amazon SNS Connector in Anypoint Exchange](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.connectors/mule-amazon-sns-connector)

## About Connectors

Anypoint Connectors are Mule runtime engine extensions that enable you to connect to APIs and
resources on external systems, such as Salesforce, Amazon SNS, ServiceNow, and Twitter.

## Before You Begin

Before creating an app, you must:

- Have access to the Amazon SNS target resource and Anypoint Platform.
- Understand how to create a Mule app using Anypoint Studio.
- Have AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) credentials.

## Next Step

After you have met the prerequisites, you are ready to create an app with
[Anypoint Studio](amazon-sns-connector-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
